steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to contrast intaglio and relief printing. The notes give a clear opposition: in intaglio, ink goes on the carved grooves; in relief, ink goes on the raised, uncarved portions. A good answer must name both techniques and explain the difference in where ink is applied.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Relief printing is intaglio’s reverse: the ink is applied not to the carved grooves of the printing block but to its raised, uncarved portions’: This directly contrasts the two by naming intaglio as the baseline and explaining how relief differs. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Relief printing, rather than intaglio, is the technique Doi used’: This identifies which technique Doi used but does not explain the difference between them. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘in contrast to relief printing, intaglio printing uses the printing block to stamp the image onto paper’: Both techniques stamp onto paper — this describes a shared feature, not a contrast in ink application. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘In both relief printing and intaglio… ink is applied to the grooves’: This states a similarity (and an inaccurate one — relief applies ink to raised portions, not grooves). ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
